title: 4. The Hindrances The Psychotherapy of Vipassana
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and systematic coverage of all five hindrances with practical meditation instructions, psychological depth, and rich illustrative examples. It uniquely frames the hindrances as 'the psychotherapy of vipassana,' offering both theoretical understanding and skillful means for working with each hindrance. The talk has excellent literary qualities including personal anecdotes, accessible metaphors, and a coherent pedagogical structure that moves naturally from one hindrance to the next while maintaining thematic unity.
summary: A comprehensive teaching on the five hindrances as the core therapeutic work of vipassana meditation, explaining how each hindrance arises, how to recognize it, and specific techniques for working with desire, aversion, dullness, restlessness, and doubt. The talk emphasizes the transformative power of observing rather than suppressing these mental states, with practical guidance on maintaining the position of the objective observer.

relevance.note: Entirely dedicated to the five hindrances teaching
